content.question: What proportion of the 22,000–25,000 students in selective gimnáziums come from disadvantaged socioeconomic backgrounds versus middle-class and affluent families, and how do these admission patterns change if admissions criteria are reformed without abolishing the tracks?
content.reduction_path: Conduct disaggregated administrative-data analysis of student intake by parental income, education level, and neighborhood; compare demographic composition before and after hypothetical admissions-rule changes (e.g., lottery, needs-based weighting, portfolio assessment).
